test.observation: Test runs but C dispatch fails with CEngineLayerSpec size mismatch: Python=1048, C=1104. System falls back to cached+stream dispatch at ~15 tok/s instead of expected ~54 tok/s.

typecheck.observation: No typecheck tool configured for this project

lint.observation: No lint tool configured for this project

blockingIssues.description: Python ctypes CEngineLayerSpec struct is missing gemv_qkv_fused field. C struct has 18 CKernelSpec fields (including gemv_qkv_fused) totaling 1104 bytes, but Python struct has 17 CKernelSpec fields totaling 1048 bytes. This causes C dispatch to fail with 'CEngineLayerSpec size mismatch' error, falling back to slow Python dispatch path (~15 tok/s vs expected ~54 tok/s).
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add ('gemv_qkv_fused', CKernelSpec) field to Python CEngineLayerSpec class in tp_engine.py between gemv_kv_fused and qknorm_q fields (line ~5408). The field order must match the C struct exactly: attn_rmsnorm, gemv_q_fused, gemv_kv_fused, gemv_qkv_fused, qknorm_q, ...

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add rule: When modifying C structs that have Python ctypes mirrors, workers must update BOTH the C struct and the Python struct definition in the same commit.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fused-qkv-kernel worker added gemv_qkv_fused to c_dispatch.c CEngineLayerSpec but forgot to add it to tp_engine.py CEngineLayerSpec, causing critical struct size mismatch.
